# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

tmp_file="/tmp/myapp-$$.tmp"
tmp_input="/tmp/myapp-input-$$.tmp"
tmp_output="/tmp/myapp-output-$$.tmp"

cleanup() {
  rm -f "$tmp_file" "$tmp_input" "$tmp_output"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

printf '%s\n' "alpha" "beta" "gamma" > "$tmp_input"

cp "$tmp_input" "$tmp_file"
awk '{ print toupper($0) }' "$tmp_file" > "$tmp_output"

cat "$tmp_output" > "$tmp_file"

while IFS= read -r line; do
  printf 'processed: %s\n' "$line"
done < "$tmp_file"

echo "re-reading the same temp file path:"
cat "$tmp_file"

cp "$tmp_file" "$tmp_output"
wc -l < "$tmp_output" > "$tmp_file"

result="$(cat "$tmp_file")"
echo "record_count: $result"
